primary_diagnosis.name: Severe oropharyngeal candidiasis with presumed oesophageal extension

pathophysiology_summary: Recent broad-spectrum antibiotics (and frailty/possible immunosuppression) have allowed Candida to overgrow the oral mucosa, producing thick pseudomembranous curd-like plaques that leave a raw, bleeding erythematous base when scraped — the hallmark of pseudomembranous candidiasis (thrush). Severe odynophagia with inability to swallow saliva in an immunocompromised/frail patient signals likely oesophageal extension, which requires SYSTEMIC antifungal therapy (oral fluconazole) — topical nystatin alone is inadequate for oesophageal disease. The acute danger here is not the fungus itself but the resulting dehydration (12 h no intake, dry mucous membranes, tachycardia, low-normal BP, reduced urine output) with risk of acute kidney injury, delirium and aspiration, plus inability to take essential medications. Discriminators: scrapable curd-like plaques over buccal mucosa/palate/pharynx distinguish candida from grouped vesicles/punched-out ulcers of HSV, from isolated tonsillar exudate of streptococcal pharyngitis, and from chronic painless adherent leukoplakia. The presentation should also prompt evaluation of why a severe infection occurred (immune status).

differential.evidence_basis: Curd-like scrapable plaques with raw base after antibiotics; severe odynophagia suggests oesophageal extension.
Can cause painful mouth, but shows grouped vesicles/punched-out ulcers, not scrapable curd plaques.
Isolated tonsillar exudate with fever/tender nodes — not widespread scrapable buccal plaques.
Chronic, painless, adherent (non-scrapable) — inconsistent with this acute painful picture.
The 12h of no intake with dry mucosa/tachycardia is the immediate danger.

differential_progression.scoring_note: Diagnose thrush with likely oesophageal extension (systemic antifungal), and recognise dehydration as the emergency. Nystatin-only for a patient who can't swallow is inadequate.

differential.evidence_basis: Scrapable plaques + severe odynophagia/inability to swallow in an at-risk patient.
Co-leading priority: dehydration from inability to swallow needs urgent rehydration.
No vesicles/punched-out ulcers; plaques are scrapable curd.
Working diagnosis: systemic fluconazole + urgent rehydration/assessment; investigate immune status.

red_flags.description: Inability to swallow fluids/saliva for hours with dehydration (dry mucosa, tachycardia, low-normal BP, reduced urine output)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Dehydration risks AKI, delirium and aspiration in a frail elderly patient; needs urgent rehydration and cannot be managed with topical antifungal alone.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Progressive dehydration → AKI, delirium, hospital admission or death.

red_flags.description: Unexplained severe/recurrent candidiasis — possible underlying immunosuppression
red_flags.clinical_significance: Severe oesophageal candidiasis can be the presenting feature of immunosuppression (e.g., steroids, diabetes, HIV, malignancy) and warrants evaluation.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: A serious underlying cause is missed.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Prescribing nystatin alone for a patient with severe odynophagia/inability to swallow (likely oesophageal), where systemic fluconazole is indicated.
Focusing on the thrush and overlooking that dehydration/inability to take meds is the acute danger.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Objective hydration status, electrolytes and renal function
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Urgent in-person assessment for hydration and to start systemic antifungal (oral fluconazole) and analgesia; ED for IV fluids if unable to rehydrate or if AKI/delirium features.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Managing with topical nystatin and home sips only, missing oesophageal extension and the dehydration emergency.

guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Oesophageal candidiasis (or oropharyngeal candidiasis with dysphagia/immunocompromise) requires systemic therapy with oral fluconazole; ensure hydration and investigate predisposing immunosuppression.
